title: Ghatigaon Sanctuary
tagline: Ghatigaon Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Madhya Pradesh

overview.short: Ghatigaon Wildlife Sanctuary is a wildlife sanctuary situated at Ghatigaon in India.
overview.description: Ghatigaon Wildlife Sanctuary is a wildlife sanctuary situated at Ghatigaon in India. Ghatigaon covers 511.0 km² in Madhya Pradesh state, west of the city of Gwalior. It extends to Tigra Dam, and includes part of the reservoir. It was established in 1981 to protect habitat of the great Indian bustard (Ardeotis nigriceps).

topPlaces.name: Ghatigaon
topPlaces.description: Ghatigaon (Gháṭígaon) is a village and corresponding community development block in Gwalior district of Madhya Pradesh, India. As of 2011, the village population was 5,641, in 1,088 households.

items.note: Ghatigaon (Gháṭígaon) is a village and corresponding community development block in Gwalior district of Madhya Pradesh, India. As of 2011, the village population was 5,641, in 1,088 households.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Ghatigaon Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Ghatigaon Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Ghatigaon Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Gwalior Airport (40 km) and the nearest railway station is Gwalior Junction (31 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Ghatigaon Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Ghatigaon Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Ghatigaon Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Ghatigaon Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.
